title: IDOR Vulnerability
description: Without knowing the system’s authorization mechanisms, it’s impossible to determine if there’s a risk of IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerabilities.  If users can manipulate identifiers (e.g., user IDs, file names) in URLs or API requests to access resources they shouldn’t have access to, attackers could potentially escalate their privileges or gain unauthorized access to sensitive data.
attack_vector: Manipulating identifiers in requests to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all API endpoints and web applications.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct identifiers.
Regularly audit access control logs for suspicious activity.
